task_completion.description: The Markups module is opened and active in the Slicer interface.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The module panel on the left shows 'Markups' as the active module.

task_completion.description: A new markup node of type 'Plane' has been created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Markups module list, there is a new entry with a plane icon or type 'Plane'.

task_completion.description: The plane markup contains exactly three control points.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Control Points table in the Markups module lists three points (e.g., labeled 1, 2, 3).

task_completion.description: The first control point is placed correctly on the Anterior Commissure (AC).
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the sagittal (yellow) view, a point is placed on the small white matter bundle anterior to the fornix columns (coordinates approx R=0, A=1-3, S=-3 to -5).

task_completion.description: The second control point is placed correctly on the Posterior Commissure (PC).
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the sagittal (yellow) view, a point is placed at the junction of the aqueduct and third ventricle, below the pineal gland.

task_completion.description: The third control point is placed on the midline superior to the AC-PC line.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the sagittal view, the third point is visible significantly higher (more Superior) than the first two points, along the midline.

task_completion.description: The plane markup is saved to the correct file path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Save Data' dialog or a file browser is visible showing the filename 'acpc_plane.mrk.json' being saved to 'Documents/SlicerData'.

integrity.description: The agent uses the Slicer GUI to place points rather than scripting blindly.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show mouse cursors, crosshairs moving in slice views, or interaction with the Markups toolbar.

integrity.description: The agent navigates the slice views to locate landmarks.
integrity.visual_evidence: Slice views (Red/Yellow/Green) change position or zoom level to visualize the AC and PC regions.

integrity.description: The agent does not copy-paste coordinates from the task description or an external file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is not seen copying text from the prompt window into the Markups table directly without visual confirmation.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The Anterior Commissure (AC) is a small white matter bundle anterior to the fornix columns. The Posterior Commissure (PC) is at the junction of the aqueduct and third ventricle, below the pineal gland. The expected distance between AC and PC is approximately 26.08 mm.
